onboarding.languageSubtitle: Pick the language for the setup and the app. You can change it later in Settings.
onboarding.phoneBody: To finish setup you need a phone: at the last step you scan a QR code to set up two-factor authentication (2FA) for your account.
onboarding.veylloTitle: Do you have a Veyllo API?
onboarding.veylloBody: Your machine hitting its limits — or want to save on power, heat and fan noise? With the hosted Veyllo API your agent runs lightning-fast in the cloud, no powerful PC required. Just paste your key below — or skip it and stay fully local.
onboarding.veylloOtherProviders: Prefer OpenAI, Anthropic, Google or your own local model? No problem — you can add more providers & keys anytime in Settings.
onboarding.veylloInvalidKey: That key could not be verified. Please check it and try again.
onboarding.veylloNetworkError: Could not reach the server to verify the key.
onboarding.veylloValueSpeed: Runs on Veyllo's servers — fast answers without taxing your machine.
onboarding.veylloValueNoGpu: No GPU, no heat, no fans spinning up — your laptop stays cool.
onboarding.veylloValueLocalOk: Prefer fully local & offline? Skip this — VAF runs great on your own hardware.
onboarding.adminAccountSubtitle: No admin exists yet. Enter the username for the first administrator.
onboarding.passwordSubtitle: Choose a password for {username} (at least 8 characters).
onboarding.errUsernameShort: Username must be at least 2 characters
onboarding.errPwShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
onboarding.scanSubtitle: Scan the code with your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator), then enter the 6-digit code below.
onboarding.manualKeyHint: Or enter this key manually in your app:
onboarding.soulDesc1: Define the fundamental mission and identity of your agent. What are the undeniable truths it lives by?
onboarding.soulDesc2: What will your agent NEVER do? Establish behavioral and ethical limits to ensure safe operation.
onboarding.soulDesc3: Describe the personality and style. Is the agent technical, professional, or creative?
onboarding.soulDesc4: How should the agent handle long-term goals and memory? Define how it evolves over sessions.
onboarding.soulNote: The soul file is saved in English so the agent reads it consistently.
onboarding.soulErrEmpty: This field cannot be empty. Please add at least one item or write your own.
onboarding.soulErrShort: Please provide more detail (at least 10 characters).

auth.serverUnreachable: Server unreachable. Make sure VAF is running (e.g. "vaf run").

welcomeGreetings: What can I do for you?
What would you like to work on?
Hey {name}, what can I do for you?
main.startConversationOrWorkflow: Start a conversation or choose a workflow
main.indexingAttachments: Indexing {count, plural, one {# attachment} other {# attachments}}…
main.attachmentsDocumentViewer: Attachments (Document Viewer) – stay in context while panel is open

notifications.title: Logs
notifications.noFiles:
No log files found.
Enable Debug Logs in Settings → Advanced.
notifications.timelineEmpty:
No timeline events yet.
Enable Debug Logs and run the agent.
notifications.toolUseEmpty:
No tool calls yet.
Enable Debug Logs and run the agent.
notifications.activityClickHint:
Click on the timeline
to inspect that moment

common.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out?

general.webSearchDesc: Optional. When set, these APIs are used for web search before fallback (scrape/DDG).
general.centralCredentialsDesc: One OAuth client per provider is used for Mail, Calendar, and Cloud. Configure once and all users can connect with one click. Add both redirect URIs in your app registration: /api/email/oauth/callback and /api/cloud/oauth/callback.

persona.ragDesc: RAG searches notes and documents. Count and relevance limit results.

ai.modelsDir: Models are loaded from the configured directory.
ai.downloadModelSuccess: Model downloaded. Refresh the list above if needed.
ai.promptCacheAutoInfo: Auto uses free RAM; fixed size overrides this.
ai.visionProviderDesc: Used when the primary model cannot process images (e.g. DeepSeek). Leave empty to show an error instead.
ai.visionModelDesc: Leave empty to use the provider's default vision model.

voice.sttDesc: Voice input via Whisper (Docker or local).

interface.darkModeDesc: Switch the entire interface to the dark theme.
interface.customCursorDesc: Use VAF's custom cursor on desktop instead of the system mouse pointer.
interface.dateTimeDesc: Timezone and display format for dates and times in the UI and in agent responses.
interface.autoOpenLinksDesc: Open links mentioned by the agent in the default browser.
interface.autoOpenOutputsDesc: Open generated files (e.g. HTML) after execution.
interface.separateTerminalsDesc: Run sub-agents in separate terminal windows (CLI/workflows).

advanced.hierarchicalIndexingDesc: For large structured documents, build a section index with LLM summaries before searching chunks. Improves accuracy on patents, contracts, and reports. Requires vector mode.
advanced.failoverDesc: If the primary provider is unreachable or errors out, automatically retry the request on a backup API or a local model.
advanced.failoverLevelOffDesc: No failover — only your primary provider. If it is unreachable, the request fails.
advanced.failoverLevelBasicDesc: On failure, fall back to a local model on this machine. Always available, no internet needed.
advanced.failoverLevelBalancedDesc: Primary, then a backup cloud API, then a local model. The right balance for most setups.
advanced.failoverLevelMaximumDesc: Full chain with more aggressive triggers — switch faster on rate limits and server errors.
advanced.failoverTimeoutDesc: How long to wait for the first response before failing over (0 = use the provider's own timeout).
advanced.failoverReturnToPrimary: Return to primary when it recovers
advanced.failoverReturnToPrimaryDesc: After a fallback, prefer the primary provider again on the next request.
advanced.thinkerProviderDesc: The Thinker runs in the background when you are idle: checks todos, automation suggestions, reminders, and can ask you targeted questions. A cheaper model saves cost without affecting main chat.
advanced.thinkerModelDesc: Leave empty for the provider default.
advanced.channelToolsDesc: Let the Telegram, WhatsApp and Discord agent use the same tools as the main agent, without per-call confirmation. On by default; protected by your channel whitelist and per-user admin rights. Turn it off to restrict channel agents to non-channel tools.
advanced.webUiDashboardDesc: Show Web UI first on startup instead of chat window.
advanced.startTrayOnLoginDesc: Launch system tray app when the OS logs in.
advanced.serverPersistenceDesc: Keep local model in memory even when no connection is active.
advanced.debugLogsDesc: Verbose logs for development and troubleshooting.

localNetwork.enableHostingDesc: Make Web UI and API reachable via SSL/TLS on the network. VAF starts an integrated HTTPS proxy (no Nginx required).
localNetwork.enableHostingHint: Other devices on the network can access VAF at this address.
localNetwork.lanUrlHint: Reachable on the local network at this address.
localNetwork.otherDevicesLanUrl: For other devices on the LAN
localNetwork.networkTopologyDesc: Devices connected to or using VAF.
localNetwork.securityWarningText: When you enable hosting, other devices on your network can access the Web UI. Ensure your network is trusted.
localNetwork.securityWarningStrong: VAF is not designed for access from the internet.

automations.noAutomationsDesc: Create schedules so the agent runs at set times.

createPopup.title: Create automation
createPopup.subtitle: Run the agent at a set time.
createPopup.errorServer: Failed to save. Please try again.

about.title: About VAF (Veyllo Agentic Framework)
about.description: VAF is a private, self-evolving agentic framework designed to run 100% locally on your own hardware. We believe that true AI intelligence should not come at the cost of your privacy. VAF is built to respect your data, adapt to your unique workflows, and grow alongside you as a digital extension of your own productivity.
about.privacyDesc: Your data never leaves your local network. With zero cloud dependencies and no third-party tracking, VAF ensures you maintain 100% sovereignty over your digital footprint.
about.localDesc: VAF operates entirely within your environment. It seamlessly integrates with your local devices and services, providing high-performance intelligence without ever compromising your security.
about.multiUserDesc: Share VAF securely with your household or team. Each user gets their own isolated environment—personal memory, preferences, and tasks—so everyone can work, delegate, and scale productivity without interfering with others.
about.memoryDesc: Unlike static databases, VAF’s memory system is dynamic. It doesn't just store information—it learns from every interaction. By recognizing patterns and understanding context, VAF proactively suggests next steps and evolves its reasoning based on your long-term goals.
about.productivityDesc: Beyond simple task tracking, VAF is a workflow orchestrator. From intuitive daily planning to executing complex multi-step automations, VAF adapts to your personal rhythm to keep you focused on what truly matters.
about.apiDesc: While VAF is local-first, it is fully extensible. We provide a robust API layer that allows you to connect to external AI providers (such as OpenAI, Anthropic, or Groq). Whether you prefer the absolute privacy of local models or the raw power of the cloud, VAF gives you the freedom to choose.
about.vafLicenseDesc: Dual-licensed. Free under the AGPL: use, modify, and distribute, but distributing VAF or running a modified version as a network service requires sharing your source. A commercial license removes the copyleft obligations for proprietary or SaaS use.
about.thirdPartyDesc: All third-party libraries and frameworks remain under their original licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0, BSD, etc.).

githubWizard.connectGitHubDesc: Enable your agent to access your repositories, list issues, and read files directly from GitHub.
githubWizard.secureDesc: VAF uses OAuth or Personal Access Tokens. Your credentials are encrypted locally.
githubWizard.twoOptionsDesc: Use browser login, device code, or a Personal Access Token (PAT).
githubWizard.oauthNotConfiguredDesc: An admin must provide a Client ID to use browser/device login. Alternatively, use a PAT below.
githubWizard.connectWithGitHubDesc: Classic OAuth redirect login (requires Redirect URI configuration).
githubWizard.connectWithDeviceDesc: The standard, most secure way to connect local agents. No redirect URI needed.
githubWizard.deviceCodeInstructions: Go to {url} and enter this code:
githubWizard.tokenHint: Generate a token at github.com/settings/tokens (repo scope recommended).

calendar.calendarUsesEmailAccount: Calendar uses your email account (Gmail/Outlook). Connect under Email, then calendar is available.
calendar.calendarConnectViaEmail: Please connect Gmail or Outlook under Email.
calendar.noCalendarAccountHint: Connect Gmail or Outlook under Email.
calendar.connectGmailOutlookSidebar: Connect Gmail or Outlook in the sidebar to see events here.
calendar.noEventsInRange: No events in the selected range.

githubDashboard.title: GitHub Dashboard
githubDashboard.subtitle: Manage connections and view agent activity
githubDashboard.noAccountForRepos: Select or connect an account to see repositories.
githubDashboard.reposErrorNoToken: No token for this account. Disconnect and reconnect the account.
githubDashboard.reposErrorHint: Check token or reconnect the account (Device Login or PAT with repo scope).

calendar.calendarUsesEmailAccount: Calendar uses your email account (Gmail/Outlook). Connect under Email, then calendar is available.
calendar.calendarConnectViaEmail: Please connect Gmail or Outlook under Email.
calendar.noCalendarAccountHint: Connect Gmail or Outlook under Email.
calendar.connectGmailOutlookSidebar: Connect Gmail or Outlook in the sidebar to see events here.
calendar.noEventsInRange: No events in the selected range.

persona.soulDesc: Define your agent's personality, rules, and behavior using Markdown.
persona.ragDesc: Max snippets (1–20). Min Relevance: only snippets with relevance ≥ this % are included; e.g. 30% = exclude everything below 30%.

intro.title: VAF is in open alpha
intro.lead: Glad you’re here. VAF isn’t finished yet — we’re testing in the open. Please keep in mind:
intro.point1: Much is still in development and can be buggy.
intro.point2: Some features may be removed, others will be added.
intro.point3: Nothing is final — behavior and the UI can change at any time.
intro.outro: Go easy on the rough edges — your feedback makes VAF better.

changelog.title: What’s new in {version}
changelog.lead: A few things have changed since your last visit.
changelog.note: You only see this when there’s something new.

editor.name: Name
editor.permRead: read — no gate, no prompt (read-only servers)
editor.permHint: External tools default to write, so the agent must plan before using them.
editor.errName: Name must start with a letter and use only letters, digits, _ or -.

editor.errUrl: A URL is required for http/sse transport.
editor.pasteHint: Paste a { "mcpServers": … } block — the form fills in automatically.

memory.noMemoriesDesc: Create your first memory to see the graph visualization. Memories are auto-connected based on semantic similarity.

userIdentity.title: User Identity
userIdentity.subtitle: Stored in user_identity.json · Timeline of agent updates
userIdentity.editHint: Edit each field below. Use one entry per line for lists.
userIdentity.viewHint: Who the agent is talking to. Click Edit to modify.
userIdentity.name: Name
userIdentity.locationHint: Location helps the agent answer weather, time, and local questions.
userIdentity.mainMessengerHint: Preferred channel for proactive messages from the agent.
userIdentity.quietHoursHint: During this window (in your timezone) the agent sends no proactive messages.
userIdentity.noUpdatesYet: No updates yet. Changes from the agent or your edits will appear here.
userIdentity.noDataYet: Load Persona & Memory tab first, or no user identity data yet.

addUser.title: Add New User
addUser.subtitle: Create a local network account
addUser.preset_standard_desc: Everyday tools, no destructive actions, all workflows.
addUser.preset_full_desc: All tools and workflows, including your custom ones.
addUser.preset_custom_desc: Pick exactly which tools and workflows.
addUser.availableToolsDesc: Select which tools this user can access
addUser.availableWorkflowsDesc: Select which workflows this user can run
addUser.enableMemoryOn: User gets a personal memory database - VAF remembers conversations and context
addUser.enableMemoryOff: No memory storage - conversations won't be saved between sessions

editUser.title: Edit User
editUser.confirmResetPassword: Reset this user's password to a new temporary one?
editUser.tempPasswordHint: Temporary password — give it to the user; they change it on next login.
